title: notogrammitis ciliata
intro: Notogrammitis ciliata, also known as the hairy fork fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of plant that is native to Australia and New Zealand, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection, and also has been linked to various health benefits such as antioxidant and antiviral activities. Notogrammitis ciliata can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
